questions.q: What were the main causes of the 1857 Revolt?
questions.answer: The main causes of the 1857 Revolt were the introduction of the Enfield rifle, the greased cartridge controversy, the economic exploitation by the British, and the socio-religious reforms introduced by the British.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the revolt in Delhi?
True or False: The 1857 Revolt was limited to the northern and central parts of India.
What was the significance of the revolt in Kanpur?
questions.answer: The revolt in Kanpur was significant because of the massacre of British civilians by the rebel leader Nana Saheb.

questions.q: Which of the following was a consequence of the 1857 Revolt? (a) The British government took over the administration of India from the East India Company. (b) The Indian National Congress was formed. (c) The British government introduced the policy of divide and rule. (d) All of the above
Describe the role of Rani Lakshmibai in the 1857 Revolt.
questions.answer: Rani Lakshmibai was the queen of Jhansi who played a significant role in the 1857 Revolt. She led the rebellion against the British and fought bravely in the battle of Jhansi.

questions.q: What was the impact of the 1857 Revolt on the Indian economy?
questions.answer: The 1857 Revolt had a significant impact on the Indian economy. The revolt led to the destruction of infrastructure, loss of life, and disruption of trade and commerce.

questions.q: The 1857 Revolt is also known as the _______________
Explain the reasons for the failure of the 1857 Revolt.
questions.answer: The 1857 Revolt failed due to several reasons, including the lack of unity among the rebels, the superior military strength of the British, and the inability of the rebels to withstand the British counter-attack.
